content_standards.statement: Demonstrate understanding of the characteristics that distinguish living things from non-living things.

indicators.statement: Identify and describe the characteristics of living things (MRS GREN: Movement, Respiration, Sensitivity, Growth, Reproduction, Excretion, Nutrition).
exemplars: Observe organisms in the school compound and list characteristics of life exhibited.
Create a poster showing the seven life processes.

content_standards.statement: Demonstrate knowledge of the properties of materials and classify them as metals and non-metals.

indicators.statement: Classify materials into metals and non-metals based on their physical properties (lustre, conductivity, malleability, hardness).
exemplars: Test materials for conductivity and sort them into groups.
Compare physical properties of iron, copper, wood, and plastic.

content_standards.statement: Demonstrate understanding of the water cycle and its importance to life on Earth.

indicators.statement: Describe the stages of the water cycle (evaporation, condensation, precipitation, collection) and explain the role of the sun.
exemplars: Create a labelled diagram of the water cycle.
Set up a simple evaporation experiment to demonstrate the water cycle.

content_standards.statement: Demonstrate understanding of the life cycles of flowering plants and selected animals.

indicators.statement: Describe the stages of the life cycle of a flowering plant from seed germination to seed dispersal.
exemplars: Grow beans in the classroom and record stages of growth.
Draw and label the life cycle of a flowering plant.

indicators.statement: Describe and compare the life cycles of insects (complete and incomplete metamorphosis) and amphibians.
exemplars: Compare the life cycle of a butterfly (complete) with a grasshopper (incomplete).
Describe the life cycle of a frog and identify the stages.

content_standards.statement: Demonstrate understanding of the structure and functions of the human digestive system.

indicators.statement: Identify the organs of the digestive system and describe their functions in the process of digestion.
exemplars: Label a diagram of the human digestive system.
Describe the role of each organ in breaking down food.

content_standards.statement: Demonstrate understanding of photosynthesis as the process by which green plants make food.

indicators.statement: Describe the process of photosynthesis, state the conditions necessary and write the word equation.
exemplars: Conduct experiments to show that light and chlorophyll are needed for photosynthesis.
Write the word equation: Carbon dioxide + Water → Glucose + Oxygen.

content_standards.statement: Demonstrate understanding of the various forms of energy and the principle of energy transformation.

indicators.statement: Identify and describe the various forms of energy (kinetic, potential, heat, light, sound, electrical, chemical) and give examples of energy transformation.
exemplars: List forms of energy observed in daily activities.
Trace energy transformations in a torch (chemical → electrical → light).

content_standards.statement: Demonstrate understanding of simple electric circuits and the properties of magnets.

indicators.statement: Construct simple series and parallel circuits and identify the components (cell, switch, bulb, wire).
exemplars: Build series and parallel circuits using batteries, bulbs and wires.
Compare brightness of bulbs in series vs parallel circuits.
